<!-- Generated by an 8-analyst repo audit (perf subsystems + Lakebase/Lakegres parity),
each finding adversarially verified against the code before inclusion.
28 findings collected, 21 survived verification. See bench/SCORECARD.md for baseline metrics. -->

# icegres — Performance & Parity Roadmap (agent audit)

## Bottom line

icegres is already fast where it counts: reads land at 6–10 ms p50 (point_lookup 6.89, filtered_scan 6.28, aggregate 6.56, join 10.43) — 6–40x faster than Trino/Spark on a single box — and eleven gated rounds have retired the obvious wins (metadata caching, I/O concurrency, single-file seed, write-buffer, COW DML, txns, ADBC/Flight, auth/TLS, ORM/JDBC/ODBC lanes). The remaining headroom is now in three narrow places, not the scan engine. First, **resource footprint and the Flight floor are near-free wins**: the server runs on default glibc malloc with no arena cap (rss_idle 80.8 / rss_peak 96.1), and the Flight accept loop never sets `TCP_NODELAY`, which almost certainly accounts for most of the 44 ms ADBC point-query floor vs 6.89 ms on pgwire. Second, **the read hot path still pays a per-query REST `load_table` probe** (~2–3 ms of every 6.89 ms read) to reconfirm a snapshot that provably cannot move mid-benchmark. Third, **the CPU-bound 5M-row path is left single-threaded** — the tuned scan collapses to one output partition and emits 1024-row batches, so decode+filter serialize on one of four cores. On parity, the honest gaps against Lakebase/Lakegres are a low-latency *durable* write (today it's 54 ms durable OR 1.28 ms volatile, nothing in between), merge-on-read deletes, and a global secondary index — the last being Lakegres's headline differentiator that is entirely absent. Several attractive-sounding ideas were refuted or de-scoped below: an NVMe/columnar data cache buys nothing on the local RustFS box and risks the size/rss gates, a Q6/`city` index speedup does not actually work (low-cardinality spread column can't be pruned), and Hudi support is a from-scratch parser that should rank last.

## Performance backlog

Ranked by impact/effort against the baseline metrics. Write-path items rank lower because the R4 single-file seed means **no gated metric moves** — they are real only for multi-file/production/S3 workloads the suite never exercises (stated honestly per item). "Gated" = one of the metrics in gate.sh; adbc_* are ungated extras but still meaningful.

| # | Lever | Subsystem | Current → target | Effort | Risk | Gate-safety |
|---|-------|-----------|------------------|--------|------|-------------|
| 1 | `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2` (+ optional mimalloc `#[global_allocator]`) | perf-footprint | rss_idle 80.8 / rss_peak 96.1 → −20–40% | S | Very low | Gated rss can only improve; env-var route touches no binary |
| 2 | Set `TCP_NODELAY` on Flight accept loop | perf-flight | adbc_query_point 44.0 → low teens | S | Low | Latency-only; no gated metric touched |
| 3 | Opt-in bounded-staleness `--snapshot-check-ms` (skip `load_table` probe) | perf-txn | point_lookup 6.89 → ~4–5; qps_8conn ~423 → up | M | Med (RYOW invalidation) | Default 0 = byte-identical; only improves when enabled |
| 4 | Plumb `with_batch_size(8192)` into the Iceberg scan | perf-read | adbc_query_bigfilter 136.9 → lower | S | Low | Larger batch only; small-table p50 unchanged (<1024 rows) |
| 5 | Raise 4 MB gRPC decode/encode ceiling to 64 MB | perf-flight | Removes ingest failure cliff; bulk_ingest ~730–830k rows/s | S | Low | Ceiling raise only; sub-4MB behavior identical |
| 6 | AtomicUsize open-txn counter fast-path (skip global mutex) | perf-wire | qps_8conn ~423 → up (8-conn lock traffic) | S | Low | Identical semantics; can't regress |
| 7 | Real scan output partitions (min(cores,files)) | perf-read | adbc_query_bigfilter 136.9 → up to ~Nx on decode+filter | M | Med (limit slicing) | Single-file tables stay G=1; small-table p50 structural no-op |
| 8 | Cache LogicalPlan across the 3 prepared-stmt RPCs | perf-flight | adbc_query_point/bigfilter −2–4 ms planning | M | Low | Fewer planner passes; prepared-path half is safe, statement-LRU risky |
| 9 | `panic = "abort"` in profile.release | perf-footprint | binary_size 125.94 MB → −single-digit MB | S | **Med (crash semantics)** | Binary can only shrink; MUST re-run full 110-assertion e2e |
| 10 | UPDATE: write one mixed-status manifest, not two | perf-write | −1 PUT/UPDATE commit (untracked; S3-relevant) | S | Med (reader parity) | Must verify Trino/Spark/DuckDB parity before keeping |
| 11 | Parallelize the 4 serial commit PUTs (tokio::join!) | perf-write | UPDATE commit 4→3 PUTs (untracked; ~RTT on S3) | M | Low | Identical objects; append insert_single untouched |
| 12 | PK-enforce reads: ProjectionMask key-columns-only | perf-write | −(total_cols/pk_cols)× decode under `--enforce-pk` (untracked) | M | Low | `--enforce-pk` opt-in; default insert path untouched |
| 13 | Bounded-concurrency COW file scan (`buffered(N)`) | perf-write | multi-file UPDATE/DELETE sum→max (untracked) | L | Low | Single-file seed = no metric moves; cap N bounds rss |
| 14 | Reuse SessionContext / arrow-compute DELETE eval | perf-write | −O(files) ctx construction on DML (untracked) | M | Low | Internal refactor; same survivor batches |
| 15 | CompatHook `ControlFlow::Break` on first user relation | perf-wire | micro CPU/statement (unmeasurable vs 7 ms I/O floor) | S | None | Semantically identical; cleanup, not a perf win |

**Top 5 detail:**

1. **Allocator/arena footprint** — No `#[global_allocator]` anywhere in `icegres/` and no `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX` in `bench/bench.sh` or `infra/scripts/flightsql-start.sh`; on a 4-core box glibc opens up to ~32 arenas that retain freed Arrow buffers per-arena. Export `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2` in the launch scripts first (zero-code, zero-binary), A/B, and only add mimalloc if it under-delivers. The vendored sibling `infra/.../lakekeeper-bin/src/main.rs:13` already uses `tikv_jemallocator` for this exact reason — the fix was applied everywhere except the product binary.

2. **Flight `TCP_NODELAY`** — `icegres/src/flight.rs:1076` hands tonic a raw `accept()`'d `TcpStream` via `serve_with_incoming_shutdown` (flight.rs:1062); tonic's builder nodelay is documented-ignored on that path, so Nagle stays ON. `icegresd.rs:1160` already calls `set_nodelay(true)` — mirror it: `.map(|(s,_)| { let _ = s.set_nodelay(true); s })`. The 44.0-vs-6.89 (~37 ms) gap is the textbook Nagle+delayed-ACK loopback stall for a small-req/small-resp 3-RPC handshake.

3. **Per-read `load_table` probe** — `icegres/src/cache.rs:159` runs `self.catalog.load_table(...)` unconditionally at the head of `current_provider()`, before the warm-cache hit at cache.rs:180-187 — ~2–3 ms of REST latency on every read to reconfirm a snapshot that is static for the whole qps window (writes run after qps, harness main.rs:632-659). Add an `AtomicU64 last_probe_at` and a `--snapshot-check-ms N` flag (default 0 = today) wired through `context.rs:120`; invalidate from local commit paths so RYOW/`freshness` stay exact and only external writers are bounded-stale. This is the single largest gated-latency+qps lever.

4. **Scan `batch_size` never plumbed** — `scan.rs:130` builds the scan with only `.with_concurrency_limit`; `iceberg-0.9.1` defaults `batch_size: None` → parquet's 1024-row fallback, which is why the R3 round measured `datafusion.execution.batch_size` as a no-op (the SessionConfig value never reaches the reader). Add `.with_batch_size(Some(8192))` with an `ICEGRES_SCAN_BATCH_SIZE` escape hatch mirroring `scan_concurrency()`. ~8x fewer batches on the 5M path; demo.trips (<1024 rows) is unaffected, so small-table p50 can't regress. Note the module doc claims aggregate/join are I/O-bound, so the honest target is adbc_query_bigfilter, not the small-table metrics.

5. **4 MB gRPC ceiling** — `flight.rs:1061` registers `FlightServiceServer::new(service)` with no `max_decoding_message_size`, so any DoPut IPC chunk >4 MB is rejected (the task explicitly flagged this). Wrap with `.max_decoding_message_size(64<<20).max_encoding_message_size(64<<20)`. Pure ceiling raise, sub-4MB behavior identical, removes a hard failure mode and unblocks larger single-batch DoGet.

## Feature parity & trade-offs vs Lakebase and Lakegres

| Capability | Lakebase | Lakegres | icegres today | Gap | Worth-building verdict |
|-----------|----------|----------|---------------|-----|------------------------|
| Fast **and** durable write | Externalized quorum WAL: low-latency + durable | (analytics engine) | 54 ms durable (full Iceberg commit) OR 1.28 ms **volatile** buffer (buffer.rs:17-22 — unclean kill loses acked rows) | No low-latency durable path | **BUILD (L)** — compute-local append-only WAL + group fsync; the cheapest honest increment. Explicitly NOT a Neon rebuild (no GetPage@LSN). Caveat: covers process-kill/OOM on the same volume, not disk/node loss or a scale-to-zero redeploy onto ephemeral storage — WAL dir must be persistent |
| Merge-on-read deletes | yes | Iceberg v3 deletion vectors | Copy-on-write **full-file rewrite** (overwrite.rs:987-1005); refuses tables with existing delete manifests (overwrite.rs:954) | 1-row DELETE on 5M file rewrites 5M rows | **BUILD later (L)** — emit v2 positional delete files. Reader already applies them (iceberg reader.rs:634). Caveats: iceberg-rust 0.9.1 ships **no** position-delete writer (hand-roll required; the `add_delete_file` cited in evidence is a COW-status name collision, not a v2 writer), and MoR shifts cost onto gated read latency — needs a paired compaction story |
| Global secondary index | (OLTP btree) | **Headline**: key→file/row-group index turns full scans into point lookups | None (grep: zero bloom/puffin/index) — pruning is 100% Iceberg manifest min/max | Equality/IN on non-clustered keys = full scan | **BUILD (XL), strategic** — opt-in sidecar index maintained via the snapshot-change hook in cache.rs. Honest correction: **does NOT speed Q6/`city`** (20-value zipf spread across all 10 files → bloom returns "maybe" everywhere). Targets high-selectivity equality/IN keys; add a spread-selective benchmark since none exists. Feasibility risk: 0.9.1 `TableScanBuilder` has no restrict-to-files setter — prototype augmented-Predicate vs custom scan first |
| Full Postgres surface | "full Postgres" bar | (partial) | XFAILs: DECLARE/FETCH cursors (none), extended-protocol SELECT-in-txn returns 0A000 (txn.rs ~924-932) | Real drivers hit both (setFetchSize, named cursors, stream_results) | **BUILD (L)** — buffered cursor iterator off existing pinned-snapshot txn state; thread portal Format into TxnHook. Additive, off the hot path |
| Lakehouse metadata introspection | (pg_catalog) | (Iceberg meta tables) | `$snapshots`/`$manifests` pass through; `select *`/`count(*)` fail on Map summary column (SCORECARD C5) | BI/observability `SELECT *` errors | **BUILD (M), cheap** — re-project Map→JSON via the collect+MemTable pattern in compat.rs:252-263. Closes a caveat *inside* an existing PASS, not a new area |
| Columnar/NVMe data cache | Memory+NVMe hierarchy keeps S3 off hot path | foyer-style column-chunk cache | Metadata/manifest cache only (cache.rs) — data re-read each scan | Object storage on hot path | **DEFER** — on local RustFS (~1 ms/GET) OS page cache already hides this; a footer cache moves no gated metric and foyer risks the binary +10% / rss +25% gates. Revisit only against real high-latency S3 |
| Multi-format (Hudi) reads | — | Hudi **and** Iceberg in place | Iceberg-only (grep: zero hudi) | No Hudi | **DEFER, rank last** — no Hudi reader in the pinned crate matrix; from-scratch timeline/base-file parser, XL speculative |

**The two strategic halves.** The study's thesis is that a complete platform wants both an OLTP-class write path and analytics-class serve-in-place reads. The best Lakebase-per-effort increment is the **compute-local WAL**: it directly converts the buffered path from "fast but volatile" to "fast and crash-durable," filling the one cell icegres structurally lacks, at L effort and fully opt-in (default synchronous path byte-for-byte unchanged, so no gate moves). The best Lakegres-per-effort increment is the **global secondary index** — it is the single named Lakegres differentiator that is completely absent, and even though it is XL, nothing else closes the "point lookups over object storage" gap. If a cheaper Lakegres down-payment is wanted first, the **metadata-table Map fix (M)** is the highest parity-per-effort quick win, but it closes a caveat rather than a differentiator. Recommended posture: ship the WAL and the metadata fix in the near term, and treat the secondary index as the flagged Phase-2 build once its 0.9.1 feasibility is prototyped.

## Sequenced next 3 candidates (gated rounds, in order)

1. **Round "footprint + Flight floor"** — `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2`, Flight `TCP_NODELAY`, and the 64 MB gRPC ceiling (all S, backlog #1/#2/#5). *Rationale:* highest impact/effort in the whole audit — moves the two gated rss metrics 20–40%, collapses the ~37 ms ADBC point-query floor, and removes an ingest failure cliff, all with essentially no code risk. *Gate risk:* near-zero; rss can only improve, latency only improves, binary untouched. Validate `qps_8conn` stays within −10% under `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX` (expected negligible on an 8-conn workload).

2. **Round "read hot path"** — `--snapshot-check-ms` staleness window, scan `batch_size` plumbing, and the atomic txn-counter fast-path (backlog #3/#4/#6). *Rationale:* the staleness window is the largest gated-latency+qps lever (removes ~2–3 ms of every 6.89 ms read), batch_size attacks the 136.9 ms bigfilter, and the atomic gate trims shared-mutex traffic under concurrency. *Gate risk:* concentrated in RYOW correctness — `last_probe_at` must be invalidated from **all** local commit paths (inserts flow through `write_delegate.insert_into`, not `current_provider`), or the `freshness` metric regresses under the flag; keep default 0 and gate on the full e2e.

3. **Round "CPU-parallel 5M scan"** — real scan output partitions across min(cores, files) (backlog #7). *Rationale:* with batch_size already plumbed, this is the remaining lever on the CPU-bound 5M filter+aggregate — up to ~4x on decode+filter that currently serialize on one core while three idle. *Gate risk:* medium — single-file seed tables stay G=1 so small-table p50 is structurally safe, but per-partition limit slicing (scan.rs:180-197) must be re-applied *after* a merge, and `plan_files()` must be cheap enough at execute time; verify the measured delta rather than assuming a full 4x, since ~44 ms of the bigfilter budget is the Flight gRPC floor that partitioning cannot touch.

---

## Execution log — R12 (first round off this roadmap)

Implemented the safe, measurable performance backlog and ran the full gate
protocol. All landed changes gate-PASS against a **same-box control** (the box
drifted ~1.6× slower than the 9-hour-old `baseline.json`, so the honest
comparison rebuilds the pre-change binary and re-benches it now).

**Kept (5):** Flight `TCP_NODELAY` (#2) — **adbc_query_point 44.0→12.8 ms, −71%**,
the round's headline; 64 MB gRPC ceiling (#5); scan `batch_size` 1024→8192 (#4,
env `ICEGRES_SCAN_BATCH_SIZE`); `AtomicUsize` open-txn fast-path (#6); Flight
prepared dataset-schema cache (#8, safe half — `GetFlightInfo` only, `DoGet`
still re-plans); plus `compat.rs` visitor break-early. e2e 110/110, parity
31 PASS / 0 GAP / 1 NA, rss improved, binary flat.

**Reverted (2, caught by the gate):**
- `MALLOC_ARENA_MAX=2` (#1) — **−42% qps under 8 connections** for a ~2 % rss
  win. The roadmap's "expected negligible on an 8-conn workload" was wrong: two
  arenas throttle the multi-threaded DataFusion executor. Removed.
- scan `with_row_selection_enabled` default-on — errors *"Parquet file metadata
  does not contain a column index"* on icegres-written files (iceberg-rust's
  writer emits no page index). Flag kept, **default off**; enabling it needs the
  writer to emit a page index first (a separate change).

**Deferred (4, each its own future round):** scan output-partition parallelism
(#7 — wrong-result risk on LIMIT-across-partitions, needs result-equivalence
tests); `panic=abort` (#9 — turns one panicking connection into a whole-server
abort, a resilience regression); `--snapshot-check-ms` (#3 — RYOW-critical,
default-off so no gated movement, belongs in its own round with a staleness
test); write-path items #10–14 (invisible on the single-file seed; multi-file /
real-S3 only).

**Method note:** the apparent read regression vs `baseline.json` (+60–75 %) was
pure box drift — the *identical pre-R12 code* re-benched now shows the same
slowdown. `baseline.json` was re-established from two agreeing R12 runs (worst
disagreement 10.3 % < the 25 % SPEC bar) on the current box.
